---
title: "Portfolio Optimisation and Risk Analytics Using Exchange-Traded Funds"
subtitle: "An End-to-End Quantitative Investment Analysis in R"
author: "Elliot Young"
date: today

format:
  html:
    theme: flatly
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show R Code"
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    tbl-cap-location: top
    smooth-scroll: true
    embed-resources: true

execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false

editor: visual
---

# Portfolio Optimisation and Risk Analytics

This project develops a fully reproducible portfolio optimisation framework using nine Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs).

The workflow integrates:

- Data collection from Yahoo Finance
- Data preprocessing
- Exploratory data analysis
- Risk and return analysis
- Portfolio optimisation
- Efficient frontier construction
- Historical backtesting
- Monte Carlo simulation
- Automated reporting using Quarto

The entire project is implemented in **R**, allowing every result, table and figure to be reproduced automatically.

# Executive Summary

This project investigates portfolio construction using nine diversified Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs) representing different investment styles and market capitalisations.

Historical market data are downloaded directly from Yahoo Finance and processed into continuously compounded returns. The project applies modern portfolio theory to evaluate multiple portfolio allocation strategies, including:

- Equal Weight Portfolio
- Minimum Variance Portfolio
- Maximum Sharpe Ratio Portfolio

Risk is evaluated using volatility, Sharpe Ratio, Sortino Ratio and Maximum Drawdown, while diversification benefits are examined through covariance and correlation analysis.

Portfolio performance is further assessed through historical backtesting and Monte Carlo simulation, providing insights into expected long-term investment outcomes under different market conditions.

All analyses, figures and tables are generated automatically through a fully reproducible workflow in R.

# Introduction

## Background

Portfolio construction is a fundamental problem in quantitative finance. Investors seek to maximise expected returns while controlling investment risk through diversification across multiple assets.

Modern Portfolio Theory (MPT), introduced by Harry Markowitz, demonstrates that combining assets with imperfect correlations can reduce overall portfolio risk without necessarily sacrificing expected return.

This project applies these principles to construct and evaluate diversified ETF portfolios using historical market data.

## Objectives

The objectives of this project are to:

1.  Download historical ETF prices from Yahoo Finance.
2.  Prepare and clean financial return data.
3.  Perform exploratory data analysis.
4.  Measure portfolio risk and return characteristics.
5.  Estimate covariance and correlation structures.
6.  Construct optimal portfolios using Modern Portfolio Theory.
7.  Visualise the Efficient Frontier.
8.  Evaluate portfolio performance through historical backtesting.
9.  Assess future portfolio behaviour using Monte Carlo simulation.
10. Produce a fully reproducible investment analytics report.

## Project Workflow

The project follows the workflow below.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR

A[Download ETF Data]
-->B[Prepare Returns]
-->C[Exploratory Analysis]
-->D[Risk & Return Analysis]
-->E[Covariance Analysis]
-->F[Portfolio Optimisation]
-->G[Efficient Frontier]
-->H[Backtesting]
-->I[Monte Carlo Simulation]
-->J[Automated Report]
```
